question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBalanceChange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a consensusEnd that is 1 nanosecond before a block boundary and a consensusStart that is 1 nanosecond after the previous block boundary, causing the balanceChangeBetween query to scan a range that spans the maximum number of crypto_transfer rows, producing a query that runs for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an alias that is a valid but extremely long byte sequence to getCryptoEntity(), causing the selectCryptoEntityByAlias query to perform a slow byte-comparison scan on the alias column in both entity and entity_history tables, consuming enough DB time to delay block processing by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the hErrors.ErrNodeIsStarting return path in getLatestBalanceSnapshot() by supplying a consensusEnd that is before the first account_balance snapshot, causing the Rosetta API to report the node as starting and blocking all block-dependent transaction processing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound errors are silently swallowed (only logged, not returned) to cause getLatestBalanceSnapshot() to use lower_bound=0 and scan all historical account_balance data, producing a query that delays block processing by 500% or more of average block time without any error being surfaced to the caller?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a consensusEnd value that is exactly math.MinInt64 to RetrieveBalanceAtBlock(), causing getInclusiveInt8Range(consensusEnd, consensusEnd) to produce a range with lower=upper=math.MinInt64, which when used in the latestBalanceBeforeConsensus query causes an unexpected DB behavior that stalls block processing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a consensusEnd that causes the selectCryptoEntityByAlias UNION ALL query to return rows from both entity and entity_history with the same timestamp_range, causing the 'order by timestamp_range desc' to produce a non-deterministic ordering and return the wrong entity state, leading to incorrect block balance calculations that delay transaction finalization by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBalanceChange()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where consensusStart > consensusEnd in getBalanceChange() (e.g., by triggering the deleted entity path with a very early deletion timestamp), causing the balanceChangeBetween query (consensus_timestamp > @start AND consensus_timestamp <= @end) to scan an inverted range and return unexpected results that corrupt block state, delaying transaction processing by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hbarAmount.Value += hbarValue addition in RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() by crafting a scenario where hbarValue is math.MaxInt64 and hbarAmount.Value is positive, causing an integer overflow that produces a negative balance, corrupting the block state visible to Rosetta clients and causing transaction freezing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp that causes the 'with last_two as (select * from mirror_node_time_partitions where parent = account_balance and @timestamp >= from_timestamp order by from_timestamp desc limit 2)' subquery to return only 1 row instead of 2, causing selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound to return the current partition's lower bound instead of the previous one, and forcing latestBalanceBeforeConsensus to miss valid balance snapshots and return ErrNodeIsStarting, blocking block processing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an accountId with HasAlias()=false and an id that matches thousands of rows in entity_history (due to frequent updates), causing the selectCryptoEntityById UNION ALL query with 'order by timestamp_range desc limit 1' to sort a large result set and delay block processing by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the path where entity != nil but entity.Key is nil in RetrieveBalanceAtBlock(), causing the publicKey to be nil and the entityIdString to be set, and by flooding with such requests cause the Rosetta API to serve responses that omit public key data, making block verification impossible and effectively freezing transaction processing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an alias that is all-zero bytes to getCryptoEntity(), causing the selectCryptoEntityByAlias query to match a special treasury or system account, returning an entity with unexpected properties that causes RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() to compute incorrect balances and delay block processing by 500% or more of average block time?
